issues.main_description_html:
DOCS

discover-sidebar-layout-brainstorming.md

1. Feature Summary

The Discover sidebar currently shows three content groups:

  • Discover Bookmark
  • Discover User

Each existing group currently shows a short list of two items. The new direction is to make Discover feel broader, more curated, and easier to scan by showing one strong preview item for each major content type.

The updated Discover sidebar should contain four sections:

  • Discover Bookmark
  • Discover Post
  • Discover User
  • Discover Hashtag

Each section shows only one featured item or one compact preview group. The goal is not to turn the sidebar into a full search results page. The goal is to make Discover a starting point that previews the main searchable content types and gives users a clear path to see more.

2. Design Goal

Create a Discover sidebar that feels:

  • More complete than the current three-section layout.
  • Easy to scan from top to bottom.
  • Visual and content-led.
  • Consistent with the current Panorra Explore/Discover sidebar style.
  • Useful as a preview before users open the full Search page.

The sidebar should work like a discovery dashboard. Users should immediately understand that Panorra can be explored by bookmarks, posts, users, and hashtags.

The recommended direction is Compact Discover Preview Sections.

This means each section has:

  • A clear title.
  • One featured item or preview group.
  • A simple "See more" text link.
  • Visual content where it helps users decide what to open next.

Result

DOCS

search-sidebar-layout-brainstorming.md

1. Feature Summary

The Search page is the full browsing destination behind the Discover sidebar.

Users can enter this page in two main ways:

  • By clicking the Search icon in the navigation.
  • By clicking "View More" from one of the Discover sections.

The page should use a sidebar layout that feels connected to the Explore/Discover experience. It should not feel like a separate blank search page. Even before a user types, the page should already show useful recent content.

The recommended direction is Feed-First Search Sidebar.

This means:

  • The Search page opens with a useful list immediately.
  • The default active tab is Post when opened from the navbar Search icon.
  • The default list shows the most recent posts.
  • "View More" from Discover opens the Search page with the matching tab active.
  • Search input, tabs, and filters remain easy to reach while the list scrolls.

2. Design Goal

Create a Search sidebar that feels:

  • Fast to understand.
  • Useful before typing.
  • Connected to Discover.
  • Easy to scan vertically.
  • Built around visual content, creators, places, and topics.
  • Comfortable for repeated browsing.

The page should answer one simple question:

"What can I explore right now?"

Search is not only for typed queries. It is also a destination for browsing recent posts, bookmarks, users, and hashtags.

RESULT

issues.display_title: Issue #3086: Create Pick of The Day Detail  Sidebar Layout
issues.main_description_html:
DOCS

pick-of-the-day-detail-layout-brainstorming.md

1. Feature Summary

Pick of the Day is a curated discovery page for selected posts and bookmark collections hand-picked by the support team.

The page should appear in the main sidebar navigation near Discover/Explore. It should feel like a familiar Panorra discovery surface, but with a more editorial, proud, and trusted tone. The user should immediately understand that these items are not random search results. They are selected highlights from the team.

POTD content includes two item types:

  • Posts: single panorama/content item.
  • Bookmarks: a curated collection/folder containing several saved posts.

Both posts and bookmarks can receive a POTD badge. The badge should appear on:

  • Post detail.
  • Post timeline/feed cards.
  • POTD sidebar page cards.
  • Bookmark cards when a bookmark collection is selected as POTD.

2. Design Goal

Create a Pick of the Day page that feels:

  • Special, curated, and worth opening.
  • Proud but still aligned with the current Panorra visual style.
  • Close to the existing Discover/Explore sidebar layout.
  • Easy to scan on desktop and mobile.
  • Strongly visual, because Panorra content is image-led.

The page should not feel like a marketing landing page or a separate product. It should feel like a native Panorra sidebar destination with a small editorial upgrade.

Recommended design direction: Editorial Sidebar Feed.

This means the page keeps the Explore-style structure, but adds stronger curation signals:

  • A compact POTD header.
  • A support-team selection message.
  • Search by title, description, or location.
  • A simple switch between Posts and Bookmarks.
  • Cards with POTD badge, author identity, content summary, and location.

RESULT

layout-1

github branch : UI-UX/Create-Pick-of-The-Day-Detail

layout-2

github branch : UI-UX/Layout-Pick-of-The-Day-Detail-v1

layout-3

github branch : UI-UX/Create-Pick-of-The-Day
